title: Whitepapers List
summary: Filter-tabbed archive of whitepapers + presentations. Each item: Type#DocNum coral badge, honest version status (no fabricated peer-review chrome), download CTA. Right rail explains status labels + offers per-format RSS + citation block. REJECTS Stitch's email 'Request Hardcopy Drafts' form.

strategy.decision: Filter-tabbed list (All / Whitepapers / Presentations) with search input. Per-item: doc# badge, date, version status, size metric, download CTA. Right sidebar provides status taxonomy + cite format + per-format RSS feeds.
strategy.why: Publication archetype Lesson 4 again — multi-format pieces are first-class citizens, not tag-filtered. Stitch's WhitepapersList gives the filter+search pattern; we reject the email contact form and replace with citation affordance (more useful for the hiring-evaluator persona).
strategy.shipped: v1 doesn't distinguish whitepapers from posts. They're posts with a tag.
strategy.gap: v2 adds /whitepapers-list with format filter, per-format RSS feeds, and a citation block. Honest version status (Published vN.M / Draft / Presented) — no fabricated 'peer-reviewed' label.
strategy.question: How aggressive should the version-status taxonomy be? Stitch fabricated 'Peer-Reviewed' which we explicitly rejected. The provisional taxonomy (Published / Draft / Presented) might still be too much status chrome — could collapse to just 'date · author'.

breaksFromV1: REJECT Stitch's email 'Request Hardcopy Drafts' form per anti-rule

currentState.summary: v1 whitepapers are tag-filtered posts. No filter UI, no per-format RSS, no citation format.
currentState.annotation: v2 promotes whitepapers + presentations from tag-list to dedicated archive surface with filter, search, status taxonomy, and citation affordance.
